issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `DynamicFormField` and non-exported `FileInput`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp` with justification comment. While the private-helper pattern is documented as acceptable, `FileInput` renders input elements and has its own props interface — it could be extracted to its own file for full compliance.
issues.fix: Extract `FileInput` to `FileInput.tsx` alongside `DynamicFormField.tsx`, or keep as-is since the ESLint disable is documented and the helper is non-exported.

issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `ReceivedOrderCard` and non-exported `BuyerInfoValue`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp`. Same pattern as COMP-001.
issues.fix: Extract `BuyerInfoValue` to its own file, or accept the intentional ESLint disable since the helper is non-exported and tightly coupled.

issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `SectionItemsTwoColumn` and non-exported `TwoColumnRow`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp` with a compound-component justification comment.
issues.fix: Pattern is intentional and documented. No action required unless extraction to its own file is preferred.

issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `SectionItemsGallery` and non-exported `GalleryItemEditor`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp`.
issues.fix: Pattern is intentional and documented. No action required unless extraction is preferred.

issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `SectionItemsCards` and non-exported `CardItem`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp`.
issues.fix: Pattern is intentional and documented. No action required unless extraction is preferred.

issues.description: File contains two component definitions: exported `SectionItemsAccordion` and non-exported `AccordionItemEditor`. Suppressed with `eslint-disable react/no-multi-comp`. Consistent pattern with COMP-003 through COMP-005 in the same inline-editor directory — all four share the same justified approach.
issues.fix: Consider extracting the four private helpers (TwoColumnRow, GalleryItemEditor, CardItem, AccordionItemEditor) into separate files to fully comply with the one-component-per-file rule, or keep the current pattern since it is consistently applied and documented across the inline-editor folder.

patterns.description: All 264 non-test .tsx files analyzed contain at most one exported PascalCase component. No file exports multiple components.
No nested component definitions found. No component is defined inside another component function body.

'use client' directive is correctly applied on all 193 client-side components. No server component misuses client hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRouter, etc.). No 'use client' component mixes async server patterns.
All error.tsx and global-error.tsx files correctly include 'use client' (required by Next.js App Router).
All providers.tsx files correctly include 'use client'.
All root page.tsx redirect files correctly omit 'use client' and use server-side redirect() pattern.

The inline-editor directory in studio app consistently uses a private-helper compound component pattern across 4 files (SectionItemsTwoColumn, SectionItemsGallery, SectionItemsCards, SectionItemsAccordion). This pattern is deliberate, consistently applied, and documented via ESLint disable comments. Consider formalizing this pattern in the component-patterns rule if it should be allowed more broadly.
